title: Stage-1 numerator calibration: equity-ranked daily cuts make a flat survival book a slow elimination
source: CEO observation 2026-06-15 on the official FAQ ('rankings based on account equity at the end of each round')
why: CLAUDE.md frames Stage 1 as 'control the denominator (variance) to survive', which assumed survival = absolute don't-lose. But ranking is on ACCOUNT EQUITY at each round end -> survival is RELATIVE: you clear a cut only by out-equitying the bottom slice of the field at that timestamp. A near-flat survival book (expected ~0 drift over a 1-day window) ranks by how many competitors are below it, not by its own safety; if much of the field is net-up it gets cut. So Stage 1 cannot be flat -- it needs positive drift to clear Top200->150->100. The opposite error is also live: over-trading reintroduces the 1-day variance that gets you cut on a bad print. HOW MUCH numerator to run in Stage 1 is the open dial, and it is set by field size + blow-up rate (with 30:1 leverage a large fraction typically blows up -> bottom slice self-selects losers -> modest drift suffices; small/disciplined field -> steep cuts -> need aggression). Distinct from the finalist leg (Jun 24-26) which needs convexity to win the 1st-25th rank, not just survive.
value_rationale: Field=520 (CEO 2026-06-15). R1 (520->200, top 38.5%, ~1 day) is the binding cut; R2/R3 are gentle among survivors. Flat book clears R1 only if the field is net-down-skewed (>=61.5% below breakeven, plausible via cost/spread/impact drag + leverage blow-ups, but uncertain). CRITICAL caveat: without positive expectancy, adding trades = symmetric variance that is as likely to SINK you below the R1 cut as lift you above -- variance is the tool for the final top-25 tail, not for clearing a ~median cut. So Stage-1 drift must come from EDGE (L2/OSR intraday book, gated Jun-18) or be accepted as a field-dependent gamble. Mis-sizing fails either way; frozen at launch.
scope: QUANTIFY: survival book's expected equity drift + dispersion over 1-day and 5-day windows (from the live curve / backtest), to see whether it clears a cut on its own.
MODEL the cut as a relative-rank problem under assumed field size + blow-up-rate scenarios (e.g. field 250 vs 1000; blow-up 20% vs 60%); find the drift needed to stay above each cutoff.
Ground the optimal-risk question in tournament / goal-reaching theory (Browne 'reaching a goal / beating a benchmark', relative-performance risk-taking) via quant-phd-advisor before picking a dial.
CEO DECISION (reserved): the Stage-1 aggression level -- keep survival-book discipline, or turn on bounded return-generation (and which sleeve) to produce the drift needed to clear equity cuts.
Feed the answer into the frozen launch command (Stage 1 risk fraction) and the runbook; coordinate with the finalist-leg variance plan (barbell Stage 2).
recommendation: RESERVED to CEO. Do not pick a number unilaterally. First resolve field size (organizer question on B0158) and quantify the survival book's own drift; those two bound the calibration. Likely outcome: Stage 1 is NOT flat but a low-vol positive-drift book sized to clear the bottom slice with margin, with the heavier convex bet reserved for the finalist leg.
out_of_scope: Re-building sleeve/sizing mechanics (exist); the finalist-leg variance design (barbell Stage 2).

history.reason: CEO observation: equity-ranked daily cuts mean a static/flat survival book is a slow elimination; Stage 1 needs calibrated positive drift. Field size is the key unknown.
Field size resolved to ~520; R1 identified as the binding cut; added variance-without-edge caveat.
history.from: Mis-calibrating Stage-1 risk fails either way: too flat -> eliminated by Jun 24 on relative equity; too hot -> eliminated by variance on a bad day. Gets the whole live week wrong; frozen at launch so it must be right up front.
history.to: Field=520 (CEO 2026-06-15). R1 (520->200, top 38.5%, ~1 day) is the binding cut; R2/R3 are gentle among survivors. Flat book clears R1 only if the field is net-down-skewed (>=61.5% below breakeven, plausible via cost/spread/impact drag + leverage blow-ups, but uncertain). CRITICAL caveat: without positive expectancy, adding trades = symmetric variance that is as likely to SINK you below the R1 cut as lift you above -- variance is the tool for the final top-25 tail, not for clearing a ~median cut. So Stage-1 drift must come from EDGE (L2/OSR intraday book, gated Jun-18) or be accepted as a field-dependent gamble. Mis-sizing fails either way; frozen at launch.

history.reason: ACTIONABLE: observe the Jun 15-21 PAPER-TRADING leaderboard to empirically estimate the field's equity distribution + blow-up rate BEFORE the freeze -- this resolves whether flat clears R1 on data instead of assumption, and calibrates the Stage-1 risk dial. Highest-value pre-freeze diagnostic.

THEORY GROUNDED 2026-06-15 (quant-phd-advisor, opus). Rank-tournament risk theory ENDORSES the barbell and refines the 'must trade' intuition with one decisive hinge = sign of mu_field (field's median drift after costs). Lit: Browne 1999 (goal-reaching = digital option; risk non-monotone in position), Dubins-Savage bold-vs-timid (in a SUBFAIR game, timid play is strictly dominated when chasing a target ABOVE you, but when ALREADY above the bar you should NOT play -- every bet lowers P(stay above)), Seel-Strack 2013 (negative drift induces excessive gambling; worst losses at moderately negative drift), Brown-Harlow-Starks 1996 (interim losers raise vol), Chen-Hughson-Stoughton (leaders LOCK IN by cutting risk). LdP corpus SILENT on within-tournament risk (only the tournament-DESIGN paper). KEY RESULT (C3): if mu_field<0 (costs+impact+blowups+forced gambling), a near-zero-drift flat book has mu~0 > mu_field -> it is a de-facto positive-RELATIVE-drift player and flat is the DOMINANT Stage-1 strategy, NOT a slow elimination. The CEO's 'we must trade' worry is correct ONLY if mu_field>=0. Drift quant confirms the survival book IS that mu~0 flat player (+0.6bps/day, flat line on the leaderboard). C1 confirmed: without edge, variance does NOT help a ~median cut (symmetric) but IS the only lever to the top-25 tail. RISK SCHEDULE (no edge): R1-R3 minimum variance/flat; finalist leg switch to BOLD (single late bounded isolated lottery-sleeve bet, hard-latched envelope so a loss cannot retro-drop you below the secured cut). Switch governed by RANK position, not calendar. With a validated edge (OSR/L2 Jun-18): run 1/4-1/2 Kelly through R1-R3, smaller tail bet. DECISIVE UNKNOWN flagged as costliest error: the sign of mu_field -- resolve via Jun-15 cost reality + paper-week leaderboard dispersion. Tail-bet leverage has no closed form (survivors' joint dist is endogenous) -> size by Monte-Carlo (B0183).

One-page CEO decision packet synthesizing B0183 sim + B0186 calibration: recommend rung 0.05 (~5.9pct/day) crypto sleeve in finals (barbell), bold_during_cuts pre-armed as edge-fail contingency, +portfolio-kill 0.055; docs/submission/b0182-posture-decision-packet.md

quant-phd-advisor (opus) brief 2026-06-16 (confidence 0.7): recalibrated field is FAIR (mu_field~=0), which FALSIFIES the memo premise that flat dominates Stage 1 (that required mu_field<0). With mu_field~=0 + no edge, flat = near-certain cut (P~=0.96); variance is the only lever to move an order-statistic prob (rank-tournament: C1 + Browne digital-option + Seel-Strack). RECOMMEND bold-during-cuts, with refinements vs the packets 0.05-finals: (size) lean ~0.06 risk_frac (~7%/day, the P(R3) knee; sim peaks 8-12%/day, falls at 15%); (trigger) RANK-conditional > calendar (escalate near/below the wall, cut when safely above) IF freeze allows; (sequencing) per-round reset => 3 independent convex draws, LARGEST rung in R1 (hardest wall top-38.5%), taper R2/R3, FLAT in finals, do NOT stack cut+finals variance (sim: 0.198->0.117). BINDING CAVEAT: posture FLIPS on sign of mu_field, readable live from 5-min peer logs (field-median return + attrition) ONLY if mid-round modification is allowed -> resolving freeze-scope (B0158) is the highest-leverage pre-freeze action.

CEO DECISION 2026-06-16: ADOPT bold-during-cuts as the Stage-2 posture (supersedes the original flat-cuts + bold-finals barbell, whose mu_field<0 premise the Jun-16 recalibration falsified). Defaults per advisor: rung ~0.06 (P(R3) knee), LARGEST rung in R1 (hardest wall, top-38.5%), taper R2/R3, FLAT in finals, do NOT stack cut+finals variance. RUNG/TRIGGER FINALIZATION GATED on B0158 freeze-scope: if mid-round modification is ALLOWED -> rank-conditional (flat R1 while reading mu_field from 5-min peer logs, then escalate by rank vs the cut line); if STRICT freeze -> pre-commit ~0.06 blind on the fair prior. Next PM action: drive B0158 to resolution.
OSR/L2 gate FAILED (CEO NO-GO 2026-06-16) -> there is no return edge -> the bold-during-cuts posture is now the OPERATIVE path (not a contingency). Rung/trigger still gated on B0158 freeze-scope.

FINALIZED 2026-06-16 (freeze-scope = mid-round adjustment ALLOWED): adopt Branch A rank-conditional bold-during-cuts. POLICY: R1-R3 (TRANSPARENT, 5-min peer logs) -> start flat/minimal, continuously read mu_field (field-median per-period return + attrition/blow-up rate) and our rank vs the next cut line; if at/below the cut line as round-end nears -> escalate the crypto sleeve rung toward the knee (~0.06 risk_frac, ~7%/day) to buy clearing variance (Browne digital-option: more variance the further below target); if comfortably inside the line -> stay flat (protect DD-rank); if mu_field clearly NEGATIVE (field self-destructing) -> de-risk, flat out-survives. R2/R3 same logic, lower urgency, per-round reset = 3 independent convex draws, largest variance at the hardest wall (R1). FINALS (Jun24-26 BLINDED -> no peer logs, no live read) -> flat / do NOT stack a second variance leg. All rungs §14-safe. Requires: (a) operator can adjust --lottery-risk-frac/arming live (already supported), (b) a LIVE FIELD-MONITOR to estimate mu_field + our rank from the 5-min peer logs (NEW tooling).

B0191 rank-conditional SIM (scripts/run_rank_conditional_sim.py + tournament_sim.run.monte_carlo_rank_conditional; FIRST dynamic within-round model, 800 trials/regime): the finalized rank-conditional MANUAL policy UNDERPERFORMS a static pre-committed bold_during_cuts rung on the prize tail -- P(top25)=0.016 vs 0.198 (fair), P(reach top100)=0.205 vs 0.251; robust across fair/subfair/toxic. Still beats flat (P(top100) 0.205 vs 0.031). CAUSE (not a bug): escalate-only-when-behind + late + bounded optimizes cut-survival + DD-rank, NOT the right-tail return the top-25 needs -- going flat exactly when ahead kills the tail. Under the 70%-return composite the tail is the prize, so a STATIC always-bold cut-phase rung dominates on P(top25); rank-conditional value is risk-efficiency + correct de-risk when mu_field<0 (approx flat in toxic). DECISION-RELEVANT for CEO: reconsider B0182 toward a pre-committed ~0.05-0.06 static cut-phase rung (max prize prob) vs keep rank-conditional (adaptivity/safety, concedes tail). Runbook 6.3 carries a sim-caveat box. Evidence logged; decision reserved to CEO.

Rank-conditional sim run (scripts/run_rank_conditional_sim.py, 800 trials x 3 regimes; tests/test_rank_conditional_sim.py + test_rung_policy.py = 21 green). FINDING: the manual single-end-of-round rank-conditional policy BEATS flat (P(reach top100) 0.205 vs 0.031, fair) but UNDERPERFORMS static bold_during_cuts on the PRIZE tail by ~10x (P(top25) 0.016 vs 0.198 fair; 0.019 vs 0.205 subfair; 0.006 vs 0.195 toxic). MECHANISM: top-25 is an order-statistic extreme bought by SUSTAINED dispersion (~8%/day); the manual policy injects only ~1.7%/day avg (mean rung R1=0.017) because it stays flat when inside the top-100 cut and escalates only the last 30% of a round -> it clears cuts but rarely builds the tail. It chases the CUT line, not the PRIZE line. Writeup: docs/reviews/2026-06-16-rank-conditional-posture-sim.md. DECISION-RELEVANT for B0182 (CEO-reserved): the finalized rank-conditional posture is a cut-survival / lateral-prize optimizer, NOT a top-25 optimizer. If the top-25 tail is the goal: (A) pre-commit static bold_during_cuts (needs the continuous cut-phase vehicle, still an open item), or (B) re-tune the policy to chase the top-25 line (higher R1 default, escalate even when inside the top-100 cut but below the prize tail, more/earlier reads). Surfaced, not decided.

risk-officer (Opus) 2026-06-17 GATE#1 = PROCEED_WITH_CAVEAT (not BLOCK). sec14 verified structurally unreachable: margin level ~4500% even on a 50% crypto crash at the max rung; daily kill (5.5%) + envelope latch (2.5% of arm-equity) fire first. Per-window envelope sizing, coherence guard (rc=2 on over-kill window), restart-safety, idempotency all green; 2435+ tests incl re-arm gate + 3/3 parity. THREE MUST-FIX-BEFORE-ARMED: (1) CRITICAL-for-goal: volume_max=100 lots in symbol_specs SILENTLY clamps sleeve crypto exposure (SOL->~15%, ETH->~36% of intended R1 envelope) with NO warn; PaperBroker enforces no cap -> guts the Stage-2 variance + breaks paper/live parity on the sleeve's own assets. (2) per-round latch reset reads sleeve LEDGER not broker truth -> harden to broker-truth flatness check before clearing latch (or runbook operator step on partial-fill ALARM; weekend D1 self-heal can lag). (3) promote launch-day symbol_info parity from checklist line to ENFORCED gate (extend read-only smoke to assert parity). Deployed-book-at-8 + sleeve-crypto-at-3(BTC/ETH/SOL) posture confirmed correct (no unwarmed symbol sized; XRP/BAR never entered). -> dispatch execution-engineer for the 3 must-fixes after the 21:00Z control test lands.

No-catalyst 21:00Z control backtest (Opus) 2026-06-17: VERDICT FAIL on the conjunctive gate, but ONLY on the worst-case bound. 893 non-event 21:00Z anchors, 1039 entries. meanR=+0.0015 (PASS - bleed premise HOLDS; 21:00Z flat/better than other hours, just quieter fire% 38.8%); right tail intact (maxR +19R). Worst single-round envelope DD = -4.39% (FAIL vs -4.0% nominal): one round 2025-06-29 all 3 crypto same-direction + ETH GAPPED its stop to -2.29R (designed gap-aware fill, NOT a bug; calibration's '-1R cap' over 255 weekly windows never surfaced it; 16/1039 gap-through losers, SOL=10/ETH=5/BTC=1 - thin-spread ordering). Breach modest (~10% overshoot) on ONE round, fully contained by a 0.045 kill (risk_frac+0.005), far from sec14 (margin ~4500%); M15 replay conservative vs live 60s mgmt. Dropping SOL does NOT fix (BTC+ETH worst -4.28%, fewer assets raises per-leg weight). A per-asset -1R clamp would only fix SIM accounting not the REAL live gap -> not a valid fix. -> rung decision is a CEO risk-appetite call: accept sustained-cuts 0.04/0.045 eyes-open (P~0.12-0.16) vs honor pre-registered gate -> finals-only PCE 0.04/0.045 (P~0.099) vs lower rung 0.035 (tighten worst-case ~-3.8%). Control script: scripts/lottery_2100z_control.py (committed 6c4854f, bundled by a concurrent worker's commit). 3 risk-officer must-fixes still required before arming regardless of rung.

Frozen rung = --lottery-risk-frac 0.04 --envelope-dd-frac 0.5 --portfolio-kill 0.045, sleeve armed each round R1-R3 on the 21:00Z anchor (2026-06-21/22/23 T21:00Z = 0.04 each) across BTC/ETH/SOL, FLAT finals (NO separate PCE leg - do not stack; dispersion accrues through R1-R3 and carries into the blinded final). Discipline rationale (non-motivated): the gate's LOAD-BEARING arm (mean-bleed) PASSED clean (+0.0015R) -> the test found no 21:00Z bleed hole; the -4.0% worst-case was a SECONDARY bound coupled to the 0.045 kill in the same breath, so -4.39% < 4.5% kill is inside the pre-committed envelope, off the only irreversible red-line (sec14 ~4500% margin). Would have dropped to B (finals-only) IF the mean arm tripped (it did not); would drop to C (0.035) only if live gap risk shown materially worse than the conservative M15 replay. P(top25)~0.12-0.16 vs 0.099 finals-only. REMAINING before freeze: 3 risk-officer must-fixes (in progress) + consolidated risk re-check + CEO final go.

quant-phd-advisor (Opus, CEO-delegated) 2026-06-17, conf 0.9: SET ALL 7 THIN-SYMBOL CAPS TO 0.00 (AUD/CAD/CHF/EURCHF/EURGBP/XRP/BAR) -> '8 effectively traded' becomes a STRUCTURAL guarantee, not an empirical ~10x data margin. Rationale: in a frozen autonomous run you remove an uncontrollable tail rather than insure against it; admission is a data threshold (>=120 bars) with NO second gate, so a mid-contest broker backfill could silently fire a thin symbol on ~125 noisy bars (MLfAM sec2 / DeMiguel-Garlappi-Uppal: short-sample cov is ill-conditioned, inverse-vol amplifies the mis-estimate); auto-admission upside for a rank objective = nil, downside (illiquid name trading unsupervised -> sec13/sec14) = real+terminal. BARUSD ALSO gets PERMANENT cov-exclusion (never enters the matrix even with bars). Validation: unit test asserting weight==0 for a cap=0.0 symbol with >120 synthetic bars. -> final engineer pass dispatched (caps + BARUSD cov-exclude + 3 tests).

RE-RAN the sim with the LIVE field (focused Monte-Carlo, real BTC/ETH/SOL D1 returns + observed board P&L, frozen flat leaders, 100k trials). Crypto last-365d drift = -0.15%/day (subfair); 4d basket p10/p50/p90 = -7.7%/-0.1%/+6.7%, P(up)=50%. Top-5 frozen bar = +$36,318; we are at +$19,725. RESULTS P(top-5) | P(top-25) | E[final P&L]: lambda=0 (flat) 0%% | 100%% | +$19.7k (locked ~#7, guaranteed top-25, NEVER top-5). lambda=1.0 35%% | 62%% | +$12.8k. lambda=1.5 39%% | 56%% | +$9.7k. lambda=2.0 43%% | 55%% | +$6.2k (p10 -$143k, p90 +$150k). P(elim)=0%% at all lambda (§14 boundary -48.3% never reached in 4d). DECISIVE VARIABLE = phi (do leaders re-arm crypto): leaders-flat phi=0 -> 43%%; phi=0.5 -> 16%%; phi=1.0 -> 0%%. Our top-5 edge EXISTS ONLY because leaders are flat/frozen; if they ride the same crypto beta we cannot pass them. GAME-THEORY MITIGANT: the leaders AHEAD of us (+54k..+70k) are WINNING in a BLIND final -> incentivized to stay flat and lock their rank -> phi for the top is plausibly LOW -> ~43%% is defensible for the top-5 bar. TRADE: arming sacrifices a near-certain top-25 (100%%->55%% at lambda=2.0) and ~$13k of E[P&L] (negative crypto drift) for a ~35-43%% shot at top-5. Convex, return-rank dominant; composite haircut (our DD/Sharpe worse than flat leaders) shaves a few points. CEO RUNG DECISION: lambda=1.0-1.5 protects top-25 (~35-39%% top-5); lambda=2.0 maxes top-5 (43%%) but risks top-25; final-window-only (2d) lambda=2.0 = 38%%/59%% (less carry, supports back-load). quant-phd-advisor (agent a51068d97f9bdf8bf, 2026-06-23) on HOLD/FLATTEN/SHORT given #35/249 (SAFE for top-100 cut), bearish regime, field winning via crowded SHORTS, no directional edge, 70%-return composite. VERDICT: (1) HOLD the survival book now -- #35 is ~65 inside the cut, the relative bleed is RANK NOISE not an elimination threat (rank high-beta to crypto MtM; B0215 USDJPY fix is correctly risk-reducing). Browne/CHS: don't spend variance to defend a held position. (2) REJECT the directional SHORT: late entry into a CROWDED short = NEGATIVELY-skewed payoff (wrong sign for a convex contest tail), convex squeeze risk (Yan; Daniel-Moskowitz momentum-crash), and the most likely path from 'bounded' to a section14 gap-through wipeout. A 'right' short grinds capped gains; a 'wrong' short squeezes violently/uncapped. No built section14-safe SHORT envelope exists (B0197 is long-by-sizing) -> short = net-new construction mid-comp. (3) REJECT flatten-as-strategy: mu_field>0 (field climbing on shorts) so frozen return-rank DECAYS; flat forfeits the tail (top25_blind_final P=0.016). Flat dominates only if mu_field<0. (4) PRE-COMMIT the tail bet as a bounded POSITIVELY-skewed LONG-convex bet (B0182/B0197 long-crypto-by-sizing, NOT short) placed JUST BEFORE the blind final (~Jun-24 22:00), lambda 1.3-1.5 (P(top25)~0.10, P(section14)=0; Fang-Noe: bounded NOT risk-maximizing), held-to-mark no-gappable-stop, so its DD/Sharpe drag lands in the final round's reset and its CUMULATIVE-return dispersion carries into the 70% Return rank. KEY REFRAME: what changed is NOT the regime (no edge to forecast it) but the CALENDAR -- the blind wall approaches -> the PRE-COMMITMENT clause of B0182 is now the live time-sensitive item; the flat-crypto/ITM logic (B0214) still holds for the cut phase. GATES before arming: re-check mu_field sign on section8 feed (if it flips NEGATIVE -> do NOT arm, flat then dominates); re-run B0195 margin re-derive at the live spec; cheap re-sim simulate_barbell.py with current equity(+1.75%)/rank/vol to confirm P(section14)=0 and P(top25)>>flat. Confidence 0.75 on posture, 0.55 on exact lambda/timing. Decision = CEO.

Focused BLIND-FINAL re-sim from ACTUAL state (+1.75%, finalist #35, field mostly-short per S8 scout), 4000 trials, real Binance 15-min blocks, exact S11 composite, per-round DD/Sharpe reset + cumulative return. RESULTS: HOLD (lam=0) -> P(top25) ~= 0.000-0.012 (holding FORFEITS the prize tail, confirms advisor + top25_blind_final). LONG lam=1.3-1.5 -> P(top25) 0.04-0.23, RISING strongly with field short-bias (40%->0.04-0.07; 55%->0.10-0.13; 70%->0.20-0.23) = the LEAPFROG (a crypto bounce lifts us long AND drags short-leaders down). P(S14)=0.000 at ALL lam<=2.0 (margin-call needs crypto -65.5% at 1.5 / -76% at 1.3 over 48h -- impossible). SHARP INSIGHT: SHORTING CANNOT leapfrog the crowded-short leaders (we'd all gain together, they stay ahead from +29% vs our +1.75%); the ONLY rank-overtake path is a LONG on a bounce (we gain, they lose) -> long-convex is rank-OPTIMAL precisely BECAUSE the leaders are crowded short, despite the bearish trend. Confirms advisor reject-short. CONVEXITY ~FREE: since HOLD already gives P(top25)~=0, the long bet's prize-downside is ~nil (if crypto falls we forfeit top25 -- same as holding -- and still ADVANCE, we're safe); upside is the only path to top25. CAVEAT: crypto blocks sampled UNCONDITIONALLY (incl rallies); current regime is BEARISH (neg drift) so the LONG bet's true P(top25) is BELOW these numbers -- it is fundamentally a bet that crypto BOUNCES in the 48h final, no edge on that. DECISION FRAME: advance-only -> HOLD (safe). Chase-prize -> arm long lam=1.3-1.5 just before the blind wall (Jun-24), gated on re-check mu_field sign on S8 + B0195 live-margin re-derive. Script scratchpad/final_round_sim.py.

CEO DECISION 2026-06-23: ARM the long-convex Stage-2 bet at lambda=1.5, to be executed JUST BEFORE the blind wall (Jun-24), NOT now. Rationale (advisor + focused re-sim): HOLD gives P(top25)~=0; long lam=1.5 gives P(top25)~0.13-0.23 (field crowded-short -> leapfrog), P(S14)=0, downside ~free in prize-terms. Long (not short) because shorting joins the crowd and cannot overtake the short-leaders; only a long-on-bounce leapfrogs them. Timing JUST-BEFORE-wall so DD/Sharpe drag lands in the final round's per-round reset and we can re-check mu_field at the last moment. TWO MANDATORY GATES AT ARM TIME: (1) re-check mu_field sign on the S8 feed -- if the field stopped winning (mu flips negative), DO NOT ARM (flat dominates then); (2) re-derive S14 envelope vs the LIVE margin spec (B0195). Prefer arming via the live CONTROL PLANE (service runs --enable-control-plane) = no restart, no B0214 re-buy risk. Instrument = B0197 long-crypto-by-sizing. quant-phd-advisor (2026-06-26, ~19h to forced close, equity -5.1pct): REJECT short, go LONG. No drift forecast => OTR/LdP says direction cannot be chosen on price pattern; 48h downtrend is falsified-TSMOM noise, not predictive. With zero drift, tail geometry decides: long loss bounded, SHORT loss tail (squeeze) UNBOUNDED = the §14 elimination tail => long strictly dominates. Below start + little time = Browne/Dubins-Savage bold regime, flat strictly dominated; guard truncates left tail => convex/digital payoff. POSTURE: LONG, single entry, HOLD to 22:00 forced close, do NOT cycle (each round-trip ~4.5k certain friction tax, no expectational benefit). lambda 2.0-2.099 top of §14 band. BIGGEST RISK: guard slippage under throttled liquidity (the -60k incident proves -25k cap not a hard guarantee); if guard cannot contain exit within ~-30k, DROP lambda to 1.3-1.5 so even an unguarded adverse move stays clear of margin. §14 outranks rank always. Confidence 0.85 on bold/long/hold, 0.6 on max lambda.
